article_meta.title: Cartoon Carousel The nation’s cartoonists on the week in politics Every week political cartoonists throughout the countr

reasoning: The model flagged many genuine instances from the author’s prose but used nonstandard bias labels, included a quoted passage in one flagged span (attribution violation), and missed several clear loaded phrases and headline claims in the author’s voice.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: One flagged excerpt bundled a Buckley quotation (“She stood resolutely…St. Francis of Assisi”) within the biasedText, violating the attribution rule; others were author prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are defensibly biased author phrasing (e.g., hyperbole, evaluative adjectives, causal assertions). Few, if any, are clearly neutral.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed notable author-level bias: headline/lede claims (“You Can Thank This Woman,” “McCarthyism is back”), “hard-knuckled tactics,” “ignominious death,” “perpetually restless and combative Joe,” “wild accusation,” and “unscrupulous investigations.”
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels like Loaded Language, Labeling/Name-calling, Speculation Presented as Fact, etc., are not in the allowed set. Many should map to Word Choice, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, Sensationalism/Emotionalism, Opinion Statements Presented as Fact, Mind Reading, or Slant.
